Patch Set 7: Code-Review-1

Sorry I left some comments on patch set 6. Please fix it in a new patch.

Patch Set 7:

Do you have an example of the JSON output being generated?  It would be great to include that in a comment block above llapi_hsm_log_ct_progress() so it is easlier to get an idea what is going on. Is the output also valid YAML?

My main concern, as I mentioned in patch #5, is that the Dynamic LNet Config project is including a YAML parse/output library, and it doesn't make sense to duplicate this infrastructure, which will lead to more code complexity for little benefit.

If the output is also valid YAML, then we still have the option to convert this functionality over to the YAML library when that feature is landed.

Patch Set 7:

Jinshan: did'nt you skip patch-set #6 ??

Patch Set 7:

Do you have an example of the JSON output being generated?  It would be great to include that in a comment block above llapi_hsm_log_ct_progress() so it is easlier to get an idea what is going on. Is the output also valid YAML?

My main concern, as I mentioned in patch #5, is that the Dynamic LNet Config project is including a YAML parse/output library, and it doesn't make sense to duplicate this infrastructure, which will lead to more code complexity for little benefit.

If the output is also valid YAML, then we still have the option to convert this functionality over to the YAML library when that feature is landed.

Patch Set 7:

I totally agree with you Andreas. 

Currently, YAML is more appropriate for config stuff, JSON is better for message exchange. It is also true that YAML is a superset of JSON.
But it is really a pity that 2 different code handling this is added to Lustre.
Moreover, in userspace, there is libyaml which is widely available and will not be a big constraint if added as a requirements.

Patch Set 7:

Example JSON event:

{"event_time": "2014-02-25 20:39:59 -0500", "event_type": "REGISTER", "archive": 0, "mount_point": "/mnt/lustre", "uuid": "696ffcba-e954-e308-8216-5c0db0dcb95f"}

This parses as valid YAML (example using PyYAML, which binds to libyaml under the covers):

In [3]: event = yaml.load('{"event_time": "2014-02-25 20:39:59 -0500", "event_type": "REGISTER", "archive": 0, "mount_point": "/mnt/lustre", "uuid": "696ffcba-e954-e308-8216-5c0db0dcb95f"}')

In [4]: event
Out[4]:
{'archive': 0,
 'event_time': '2014-02-25 20:39:59 -0500',
 'event_type': 'REGISTER',
 'mount_point': '/mnt/lustre',
 'uuid': '696ffcba-e954-e308-8216-5c0db0dcb95f'}

Patch Set 6:

(6 comments)

Patch Set 7:

As a proof-of-concept, I've been able to make PyYAML emit JSON-compatible output with a custom emitter. Looking at figuring out how to do it with libyaml in C now.
Uploaded patch set 8.

commit.subject: LU-4020 hsm: allow copytool event monitoring with JSON
commit.message:
LU-4020 hsm: allow copytool event monitoring with JSON

Adds hooks into various llapi_hsm_* functions to emit JSON-formatted
event messages for consumption by a monitoring agent. The copytool
needs to be supplied with an optional --event-fifo argument to
enable this feature.

Incorporates the following work done by Bruno Faccini:
* Put all JSON routines in a separate file/lib to allow for
  LGPL licensing.
* Major code cleanup to follow best practices and address review
  concerns.

Signed-off-by: Michael MacDonald <michael.macdonald@intel.com>
Signed-off-by: Bruno Faccini <bruno.faccini@intel.com>
Change-Id: I1fecfb8e60ef091c6d37d16dc1faf40be45c5ee2
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/7790
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
